what_it_does: Renders a share panel for a virtual hub, showing a shareable URL and buttons for Email, Copy link, Embed, and Download PDF brochure, plus a note that recipients see the current published stage.
observations: Defines a small ShIcon helper that renders an SVG from an array of path 'd' strings.
SharePanel is an aside with a close button, title, URL, four share action rows, and a muted footnote.
The share URL hardcodes the base 'https://conceptv.io/panotours/acme/' and falls back to 'tower-east' if hub.id is missing.
Each share row is a button containing an ShIcon and a text label.
The component is exposed globally via window.SharePanel = SharePanel.
The fine-print paragraph uses an inline style object rather than a class.
standouts: SVG icons are hand-coded as path-string arrays passed to a tiny local component.
The share URL embeds a hardcoded tenant slug 'acme' and a fallback hub id.
The component registers itself on window, suggesting a non-module build target.
conventions: Class names use the 'vh-' prefix consistent with a virtual-hub UI kit.
Optional chaining is used for hub?.id.
Breaks typical encapsulation by assigning to window inside the component file.
concerns: Hardcoded tenant slug 'acme' and fallback hub id 'tower-east' limit reuse.
SVG icons lack accessibility attributes such as aria-hidden or title.
Share buttons have no visible onClick handlers wired in this file.
Global window assignment can cause naming collisions in larger apps.
Inline styling for the footnote bypasses the design-system CSS classes.
notes: A small, self-contained share panel with inline SVG icons and a global export, but it hardcodes tenant and fallback identifiers.
questions: Is 'acme' intended as a placeholder tenant slug or a permanent default?
Should the share action buttons invoke handlers passed via props, or are they meant to be wired elsewhere?
Is the panel intended as a modal overlay or an inline sidebar?
Why is the component assigned to window rather than exported as a module?
purpose_vs_actual: The file's purpose is to provide a share panel for a hub, and its content matches that purpose.
apparent_themes: Global component registration for simple integration
Share affordances for published hub state
intention_signals: It appears designed to give users a quick, lightweight way to distribute a hub via email, link, embed, or PDF, while warning that the shared view reflects the current published stage.
novelty: The ShIcon component turns arrays of path strings into SVG icons, and the file exports the panel to window rather than using ES modules.
contribution: Provides a compact share-panel layout and a reusable inline-SVG icon renderer that can drive action rows without external icon libraries.
summary_for_embedding: SharePanel.jsx is a React component that renders a virtual-hub share panel with a URL, four share-action buttons rendered with inline SVG icons, and a published-stage disclaimer, exposing itself globally via window.SharePanel.
intention_for_embedding: This file was intended to provide a lightweight, self-contained share UI for a virtual hub with copy-link, email, embed, and PDF-brochure actions.

symbols.does: Renders an 18x18 SVG icon from an array of path 'd' strings.
symbols.description: ShIcon is a tiny local helper component that takes a prop 'd' containing path data strings and maps them into <path> elements inside a fixed-viewBox SVG, used by the share rows to display inline icons without an external icon library.

symbols.code_id: code://counterpart-design/reference/ConceptV Design System-handoff/conceptv-design-system/project/ui_kits/virtual-hub/SharePanel.jsx::SharePanel | component | function SharePanel({ onClose, hub }) {
symbols.does: Renders a shareable hub URL and a set of share-action buttons in a closable panel.
symbols.description: SharePanel is a functional React component that receives onClose and hub props, renders an aside panel titled 'Share this Hub', displays a hardcoded-base share URL using hub?.id with a fallback, lists four share actions (Email, Copy link, Embed, Download PDF brochure) each with an inline SVG icon, and ends with a note about the published stage. It also assigns itself to window.SharePanel.
